steps: Go to https://dev.fitbit.com/apps/new and log in with your Fitbit account
Fill in the required fields: Application Name, Description, Application Website URL
Set 'OAuth 2.0 Application Type' to 'Server' for server-side applications
Enter a Redirect URI (e.g., https://your-app.com/callback or http://localhost:3000/callback for development)
Set 'Default Access Type' to 'Read & Write' (or 'Read Only' if write access is not needed)
Click 'Register' at the bottom of the form
After registration, your Client ID and Client Secret are shown on the app detail page
Copy both the Client ID and Client Secret immediately

credential_fields.name: client_id
credential_fields.label: Client ID
credential_fields.description: The OAuth 2.0 client ID assigned to your registered Fitbit application

credential_fields.name: client_secret
credential_fields.label: Client Secret
credential_fields.description: The OAuth 2.0 client secret for your registered Fitbit application. Keep this confidential.

notes: Fitbit requires OAuth 2.0 — there is no simple API key option for the Web API
The 'Personal' app type only allows access to the developer's own Fitbit account
Use 'Server' type for multi-user applications with a backend
Intraday data (heart rate at 1-second intervals, etc.) requires special permission — contact Fitbit
Free to register; no payment required to access personal or development data
Access tokens expire after 8 hours; use the refresh token to obtain a new one

test_endpoint.description: Retrieve the authenticated user's Fitbit profile. Requires a valid access token in the Authorization header.
